mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate below 5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in UNRATE < 5% periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or the relationship breaks in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and declines in the federal funds rate create a 'stagflationary' environment where equity valuations contract, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-occurring CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction effect vanishes in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in GDP (GDPC1) above its 1-year moving average indicates expanding economic activity that lifts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in GDPC1 above-MA periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the relationship breaks in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 5-day rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing activity that boosts equity earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in INDPRO rising trends are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the effect disappears in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.8 million units indicates strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in HOUST > 1800 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the relationship breaks in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction effect vanishes in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4% during a tightening cycle raises borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in FEDFUNDS > 4% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the relationship breaks in walk-forward tests.
